[{"text": "# Meridian Robotics — Company Overview

Meridian Robotics is a fictional company invented for this test harness. It was

Meridian's headquarters are in Tromsø, with a second engineering office in Aberdeen,

sites.", "source": "01_company_overview.md", "score": -0.5506391525268555}, {"text": "# Meridian Robotics — Product Line

Meridian sells two drone models and one software product.

## Kestrel-1
The Kestrel-1 is Meridian's entry-level inspection drone, released in 2020. It has a
flight time of 38 minutes on a single charge and a maximum wind tolerance of 14 metres
per second. The Kestrel-1 carries a 20-megapixel visible-light camera but no thermal
imaging.", "source": "02_products.md", "score": -0.6974365711212158}, {"text": "The company's mission statement is: "Keep humans off the turbine, keep the turbines
turning." Meridian is privately held and has never disclosed annual revenue publicly.", "source": "01_company_overview.md", "score": -0.7732611298561096}, {"text": "# Meridian Robotics — Operations & Safety Policy

## Pre-flight checks
Every inspection flight requires a two-person crew: a pilot and a safety observer. The
pilot completes a 12-point pre-flight checklist before takeoff. If wind speed at the
turbine hub exceeds the drone's rated tolerance, the flight is cancelled — no exceptions.

## No-fly conditions
Meridian drones do not fly in the following conditions: lightning within 10 kilometres,
visibility below 500 metres, or icing conditions on the turbine blades. The safety
observer has unilateral authority to abort any flight.", "source": "03_operations_safety.md", "score": -0.8170315027236938}]
